brief_summary: Four-row categorization table of stabilizer options. Row 1: budget factory (replace before servicing). Row 2: mid-range factory (service first). Row 3: aftermarket baseline — Durock V2, TX Stabilizers (reliable baseline). Row 4: premium aftermarket — Staebies, C3 Equalz (diminishing returns vs serviced V2). Each row shows tier, mount type in JetBrains Mono, example products, and an amber verdict chip.
data_sources: stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Factory stabilizers versus aftermarket' — 'Budget-board factory stabilizers... are typically worth replacing before servicing'
stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Factory stabilizers versus aftermarket' — 'Mid-range factory stabilizers... are usually serviceable to a good result'
stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Factory stabilizers versus aftermarket' — 'Durock V2 and TX Stabilizers are the reliable aftermarket baseline'
stabilizers-explained.mdx § 'Factory stabilizers versus aftermarket' — 'Staebies (Staebilizers) and C³ Equalz represent the premium tier... The diminishing-returns curve is real'
